ety: prob. fem. of khatāʼunā, denom. of khātā
def: s.f. Ledger, account-book; the roll, volume, or paper on which are abstracted into distinct heads the several items first entered in a day-book (or a field-book); — an account made up from the khasra or field-book, a classified list of cultivated holdings prepared at the time of a village survey (in which the fields belonging to each thok, paṭṭī, and individual are brought together, and the name of the occupant, the number of each field, its extent, with deductions for waste or the like, the rate of assessment per bīghā, the total rent, and the manner of its payment, are recorded).

ety: prob. for acc. of A. ḵẖat̤ā, 'fault'
def: s.f. (Arith.), Position, or the rule of false
s.m. Asking or demanding a woman in marriage; contracting a marriage; marrying, betrothing; — a thing, affair, business, work, cause.
s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'bedstead.' s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs who are generally employed as cart wrights; a man of that caste, a carpenter, a cartwright.
s.m. = ḵẖat̤īb, q.v. s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

def: s.m. A public speaker, an orator, a preacher, a reciter of a ḵẖut̤ba, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خطیب (xat̤īb), itself from Arabic خَطِيب (ḵaṭīb).

def: s.f. The duty or office of a ḵẖat̤īb or preacher, &c.

ety: act. part. n. of خطف 'to seize,' &c.
def: part. adj. Seizing, snatching, carrying off, taking away, removing.
s.m. A hunter, &c. (see khaṭṭik); — a low caste who live principally by keeping pigs and poultry; a man of that caste; — a tanner.
s.f. Chalk (see khaṛī).

ety: act. part. n. of ختم 'to seal'
def: s.m. The seal, or the last, of the prophets or apostles, the prophet Mohammad
subs: s.m. A setter of stones in rings; a seal-setter.

ety: for A. خاتمة ḵẖātimat, fem.; see ḵẖātim
def: s.m. End, issue; conclusion, finish, finis; epilogue; appendix
subs: s.m. A happy end or issue
s.f. A kind of workmanship in making bows; — the ceiling of a roof
v.t. To bring to an end, carry to a conclusion; to put an end to, to close (a business, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَاتِمَه (xātima), borrowed from Arabic خَاتِمَة (ḵātima).

def: adj. Characterized by rank or station, eminent, noble, honourable, dignified, great, important.
wety: From Arabic خَطِير (ḵaṭīr).

ety: part. n. of خطر 'occur to the mind,' &c.
def: s.f. Displeasure
subs: adj. Displeased, offended; hurt, grieved; dejected, sad, sorrowful, melancholy
adj. Disturbed in mind, discomposed, distressed, agitated; dispirited
adj. Distressed in mind, oppressed in spirit
adj. Troubled in mind, disturbed, discomposed
adj. Pleasing to the mind, agreeable, liked, approved

adj. & s.f. Collected, composed, comforted, assured, contented, confident, tranquil, at ease; satisfactory; — collectedness or peace of mind, composure, content, satisfaction, confidence, assurance, encouragement
adv. Comfortably, easily, at (one's) ease, conveniently
v.t. To set (one's) mind at ease (with respect to, -se, or -kī t̤araf-se), to satisfy, assure, &c.
s.f. Comfort, confidence, composure, ease of mind
adj. & adv. Agreeable to wish, satisfactory; — to (one's) satisfaction or pleasure, to the heart's content
s.f. Consideration, regard, favour; gratification, satisfaction, encouragement, confidence, comfort; — hospitable entertainment, warm reception
(-kī), To show consideration (for); to gratify, indulge, treat with kindness or favour; to receive or entertain (one) warmly; to comfort, to encourage
(-kī), To have a mind (for), to like; to fix the mind or intention (on), to purpose; — to conciliate, quiet, comfort, encourage
adj. Stealing the heart, heart-alluring, &c. ( = dil-fareb, q.v.s.v. dil)
(-kī), To study the feelings or the interest (of), to desire to please, to indulge; to soothe, comfort, encourage; to conciliate, reconcile; to fondle, show affection (for), be partial (to)
v.t. To bear in mind, to remember
v.t. To allow to enter the mind; to think of, give a thought to; to regard, mind, care for, attend to (generally used negatively, e.g. ḵẖāt̤ir-meṅ na lāyā)
adj. & s.f. Impressed on the mind, imprinted on the memory; by heart; — impression on the mind; any mark of remembrance, &c.
v.t. To impress (a thing) on the mind (of, -kī), to convince
adj. Fixed in the mind or memory; by heart; — chosen, beloved
adj. (barbarous), Possessing a large heart, kind, obliging
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خاطر (xātir), from Arabic خاطِر (ḵāṭir).

def: adv. In or by the mind or memory; by heart; — willingly; — for the sake of; as a favour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خاطر (xātir), from Arabic خاطِر (ḵāṭir).

ety: khāṭ, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इया, इआ; S. aff. इका
def: s.f. A small bedstead; a bier
subs: God grant (or bring it to pass that) his bier may be carried out, or God grant be may die (a common form of cursing).

def: s.f. Account-book ( = khātā, khataunī); — entry in a ledger.

ety: khatiy˚ = khātī, fem. of khātā+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā
def: v.t. To enter in an account-book or ledger.

ety: khaṭiy˚ = khāṭī, fr. khāṭ, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. To trust, to give credit to; — to have amicable dealings with, to live on friendly terms with.
s.m. Concord, harmony, amicability, good terms.

ety: khatak; fr. khaṭaknā+Prk. अओ = S. कः
def: s.m. Knocking, rapping, rattling (of a stick, &c.); knock, rap, rattling sound or noise, clatter; sound of footsteps; — a rattle, clapper (to scare away birds = khaṭkhaṭā); a bolt, catch; — a pricking or rankling (in the mind); offence; a catch or hitch (in the mind), scruple, demur, misgiving, suspicion, doubt, suspense; perturbation, anxiety, care, concern; apprehension, fear, dread; — loss (in trade, &c.; in this sense generally khuṭkā); — a shake, or trill (in music, or in a voice)
subs: (-ko), To have a misgiving; to feel apprehension, to be apprehensive, to fear, &c.
(-kā), To listen for the sound of footsteps
adv. Without scruple or misgiving; without apprehension or fear; unhesitatingly; smoothly, easily.

def: v.t. To knock or rap at, to thump; hammer; to slam (a door, &c.); to rattle (a door, or chain, or a stick, &c.); to shoot or draw (the bolt or catch of a door); — s.m. Knocking or rapping at, rattling, &c.
s.f. by redupl. of khaṭ, q.v.

ety: see khaṭ, and khaṭ-khaṭ; and cf. S. खटखटाय
def: v.t. To knock at (a door), to rap at or on, to tap, to rattle, to make a clatter with, to shake ( = khaṛkhaṛānā).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'knock.' s.f. Rubbish, refuse, filth, dirt, dung, muck, manure; anything rotten or decayed
subs: or khāt ḍālnā (-meṅ), To dung, to manure (land)

ety: prob. khaṭ, q.v.+Prk. अडओ = S. अ+ल+कः
def: s.m. Breach, disagreement, dissension, contention; — strife; — encumbrance, impediment; possessions, belongings; wife and family, household, dependents, retinue, train; travelling domestics and things, baggage; furniture, household goods; — pile, heap; group, multitude, company; — labour, exertion; trade, business, employment; a troublesome (or intricate) business or affair; a knotty point; an affair, a matter, a case or suit.
s.m. Seal; end, conclusion, termination; a recitation or perusal of the Qǒrʼān ( = A. ḵẖatma); the feast celebrated at the end of such perusal; — adj. Done, concluded, completed, terminated
subs: s.m. The seal of those sent (of God), the prophet Mohammad
v.t. To conclude, close, finish, complete, terminate (at, -par)
(-par), To be concluded, be terminated (at), be finished.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ختم (xatm), itself from Arabic خَتْم (ḵatm).

def: s.m. A bug
subs: s.m. A flying bug, Ӕthus maurus (cf. koṭhmīr-kā kīṛā)
s.m. The scarlet flying bug, Dystercus cingulatus.
wety: کھٹ (“bed, cot”) + مل (“filth”).

def: s.f. A recitation or perusal of the whole Qǒrʼān ( = ḵẖatm).
s.f. Marsh-mallow, Althœa officinalis.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. khaṭṭā. s.m. A pit (for storing grain, or ice, &c.), a subterraneous granary ( = khāt, and khātā); — store, heap, stock.
s.m. Circumcising; circumcision; — a circumcision-feast.

ety: khaṭ˚ = Prk. खंडे(इ) = S. खण्ड ˠ(ते), pass. of rt. खण्ड
def: v.n. To be interrupted, or obstructed (a person, or a work or business); to come to a stand, to stop, to stick; to hold back doggedly or firmly, to remain firm or obstinate (in); — to put up (at a place), to stay, tarry, remain; to continue, last; to remain over, to be gained.
s.m. Circumcision ( = ḵẖatn, q.v.)

ety: khāṭ, q.v.+Prk. अउल्लओ = S. उल+कः; — or olā is a dim. affix
def: s.m. A small bedstead or bed; a rickety or dilapidated bedstead.
s.f. dim. of khaṭolā, q.v.

ety: khaṭol = khatolā+dim. aff. nā = Prk. लओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A small bed, a child's bed, a cradle ( = pālnā); — a kind of net or snare.
s.f. = H کهٿپٿيا खटपटिया khaṭ-paṭiyā, adj. & s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'knock.'
adj. & s.m. = H کهٿپٿ खटपट khaṭ-paṭ, s.f. See s.v. khaṭ, 'knock.'
s.m. = H کهٿراگ खटराग khaṭ-rāg, s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'six.'
s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: for A. خطرة ḵẖat̤rat, fem.; n. of un. fr. خطر
def: s.m. Danger, &c. ( = ḵẖat̤ar, q.v.); fear, apprehension
subs: (-se), Not to fear or apprehend danger (from), not to be afraid (of); to make no bones (of), to make no difficulty
To expose to danger, &c., to endanger, imperil, to risk, hazard, venture.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَطْرَه (xatra), borrowed from Arabic خَطَر (ḵaṭar).

def: s.m. = H کهٿپورا खटपूरा khaṭ-pūrā, s.m. See s.v. khaṭ, 'six.'
s.m. A young Khatrī, son of a Khatrī.
s.f. A woman of the Kshatrī or military caste of Hindūs; — the wife of a Kshatrī.

ety: v.n. fr. خطّ 'to make a line or mark'; 'to write'
def: s.m. A line, a streak, or stripe, a mark; lineament; — writing, character, handwriting chirography; a letter, epistle; — down on the face, incipient beard, &c.; beard; moustaches
subs: s.m. 'Sun-shaped characters,' a form of Persian writing in which the curved tails of the letters are portions of a circle
v.n. A letter to arrive; — down to appear (on the face), beard, &c. to begin to sprout
or ḵẖat̤t̤-ě-ʻětidāl, s.m. The equinoctial line, the equator
v.n. Handwriting to fall off or become worse
To shave (the chin, &c.), or to have (the chin) shaved; to dress or trim the beard
s.m. 'Egg-shaped characters,' a form of Persian writing in which the curved tails of the letters are segments of an oval
adj. Marked with lines or streaks; striped (as cloth, &c.)
v.n. Handwriting to improve or to become good
ḵẖat̤t̤-ě-shǒʻāʼī, s.m. Rays of the sun; a solar ray; — a kind of writing
s.m. The smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting
s.m. A kind of flourished handwriting
s.m. An unclaimed letter; — a release or acquittance in writing: a writing relinquishing all claim (to)
s.m. A right line, a straight line
To have the beard shaved off
To copy the handwriting (of); to forge a handwriting
s.m. Plain, round Persian writing (see nastaʻlīq)
s.m. A kind of Arabic writing (see nasḵẖ)
s.m. 'Broken writing'; a running hand in the Persian &c. character (see shikasta).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَطّ (xatt), borrowed from Arabic خَطّ (ḵaṭṭ), from خَطَّ (ḵaṭṭa).

def: s.m. A pit (for storing grain, or ice, &c.), a subterraneous granary ( = khāt, and khātā); — store, heap, stock.

s.m. (dialec.) A preparation of opium, alum, turmeric, lemon-juice, &c. levigated together in a copper vessel and heated (it is applied to the eyes in ophthalmia, to the head in headaches, and to sprains, &c.).
adj. (f. -ī), Acid, sour, tart, harsh; — s.m. (local) A citron
subs: (-kā), To taste the bitters (of); to reap the bitter fruit (of); — khaṭṭā khānā or khā-rakhnā (-se), To feel bitterness or aversion (to); to bear malice (against a person, on account of, -par or -pe)
or khaṭ-mīṭhā or miṭhṛā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Sour and sweet; — a mixed taste of sour and sweet; an agreeable taste; — khaṭ-mīṭhā, s.m. A kind of fruit (which has a sour-sweet taste)
To eat acid and sweet things; to take the bitter with the sweet, to take things as they go
To have an agreeable taste or sensation; to feel a longing (for); to water (with longing, the mouth); — to be displeased; to have a misunderstanding or quarrel
v.n. To be or become sour, to turn sour; — to be displeased or offended (with, -se)
ex: The lemon is sour to the taste.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi کھٹّا (khaṭṭā, “yellow, sour”), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀝𑁆𑀝 (khaṭṭa), from Sanskrit खट्ट (khaṭṭa). The expected outcome from direct inheritance is کھاٹا (khāṭā), so the lack of vowel lengthening before a geminate consonant means that the word was probably borrowed from Punjabi.

ety: khaṭṭā, q.v.+pan = प्पणं = S. aff. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Sourness, acidity; — moroseness, severity, harshness, sternness.

ety: khaṭṭā, q.v.+pan = प्पणं = S. aff. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Sourness, acidity; — moroseness, severity, harshness, sternness.
s.m. The civet or zebet cat, Viverra zibetha; a pole-cat; a wild cat.
s.m. A practiser of the art of writing, a good writer, a caligraphist.
s.f. A pit or cavity (for grain, &c.), a subterraneous granary, &c. ( = khattā)
subs: 'To fill a grain-pit,' to store up (grain) in a pit.

def: adj. Linear, lineal; — in writing; literal; — of or pertaining to the beard, &c.; — of, or belonging to, or made at, Ḵẖat̤ (a place in Arabia, famous for the spears made there); — s.f. A spear made at ḵẖat̤.
s.m. A hunter, one who lives by killing and selling game (see khaṭīk, the popular form).
s.m. The second (the military and regal) caste of Hindūs; a member of that caste; (locally) a grain-merchant, or grocer, a baniyā.
s.m. A labourer, hireling.
